question.text: A long straddle with 20 lots at strike 83.00 (call premium 0.20, put premium 0.15). At expiry, USDINR = 82.20. Total P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Call expires worthless (82.20 < 83.00). Put payoff = 83.00 - 82.20 = 0.80. Net per unit = 0.80 - 0.35 (total premium) = 0.45. Total = 20 x 1000 x 0.45 = Rs. 9,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 16,000 uses only put premium (0.80 - 0.15 = 0.65), forgetting the call premium was also paid. **Cross-Exam Context:** Straddle P&L at various expiry levels tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Total premium paid (0.35) is the max loss at strike price. Movement beyond BEP (82.65 or 83.35) generates profit.
